name: German Commodore Logo
description: A version of Logo made for Germans using Commodore 64 computers.

commands.description: Divides the specified integers.  Any fractional part of a result is removed.  This is similar to the / operator in WebLogo except that it does integer division.

Goes to the specified label.  Similar to a goto statement in some versions of Logo and BASIC.  WebLogo doesn't support goto statements but they sometimes translate to usage of commands like if and forever.
Indicates that a variable is local to the containing procedure.  Similar to 'local' in most versions of Logo.  Any other assignments to that variable should translate to localmake in WebLogo.
Checks if the specified name is the name of a variable

Returns the value corresponding with a specified variable name.  This is similar to the 'thing' command in some other Logo dialects.  In WebLogo, you'd add a colon prefix to the variable name to read it.
Checks if the specified value is a word.

keywords.description: dann roughly corresponds with 'then' from programming languages like BASIC.  It would mark the beginning of the code block or instruction list for an if-statement.
sonst is used in if-else statements or in German, wenn-dann statements.  It would translate to 'else' in some other Logo dialects.  WebLogo's most similar alternative is with the ifelse command.
